body.title: [awaiting_validation] Daemon supervisor — runme.py hot-reload, .borkedpy error handling
body.result: RunmeSupervisor implemented in devices/ground_loop/supervisor.py; wired into GroundLoop daemon; 12 tests pass (discovery, hot-reload, borkedpy error handling, recovery, config, stale detection, stop_all)
body.intention: I intend that Ground Loop's file-pattern supervisor is robust, transparent, and requires no code changes to add new daemons.
body.description:
Implement Ground Loop's file-pattern supervisor. (1) Discover: scan `devices/*/groundloop/runme.py`; ignore `*.borkedpy`, skip if path unchanged since last load. (2) Import: `importlib.reload()` on each runme.py; catch import errors → log to datacenter_logs/ground_loop/ and rename to .borkedpy. (3) Run: call runme.start() or equivalent entrypoint; catch runtime errors → log + rename to .borkedpy. (4) Config: load `devices/*/groundloop/config.yaml` (launch frequency, timeout, restart policy, etc.); apply per daemon. (5) Recovery: detect stale .borkedpy files (not touched in 24h) and log them for manual review. (6) Lifecycle: on Ground Loop startup, load all runme.py; on SIGHUP, reload all; on shutdown, call runme.stop() for each.

**Affected files:** devices/ground_loop/supervisor.py (new), devices/*/groundloop/runme.py (pattern), devices/*/groundloop/config.yaml (pattern)
**Design rules:** external-state-principle (state in .borkedpy filenames, not memory), log-state-changes (every load/crash/rename)
**Scope boundary:** Supervisor pattern only; Ground Loop daemon process is separate; individual runme implementations are separate
**Completion criteria:** (1) Discovers all runme.py files; (2) hot-reloads on filesystem changes; (3) renames to .borkedpy on error; (4) loads config.yaml per daemon; (5) logs all transitions; (6) graceful shutdown calls runme.stop(); (7) SIGHUP reloads; (8) stale .borkedpy detection
**Test plan:** (1) Create test runme.py, verify Ground Loop discovers it; (2) Modify runme.py, verify hot-reload; (3) Inject error in runme.py, verify rename to .borkedpy; (4) Fix and rename back, verify reload; (5) Test config.yaml parsing; (6) Verify logs in datacenter_logs/ground_loop/supervisor.log
